subject: [Email Update] Bi-Weekly CB-PM Catch Up 20250313
deck_filename: Bi-Weekly CB - PM Catch up - 20250314 (Email Update).pdf

content.answer:
Hi All,

As this week's meeting is cancelled, we will submit the meeting deck for
reading.

*Agenda:*
Key Initiatives Update

   1. CNLS seller feedback on Marketing tool
   2. Shopbot LLM Rollout Status Update
   3. CB BUG SLA review - Feb

Kindly note that

   - There will be a separate email out on 03.14 requesting for HOD
   approval on new marketplace P0P1 requests.
   - Regular OKR project update is omitted from today's email. Please refer
   to the regular weekly update email out on 03.15.

Hi Ian

Update the shopbot launch status on page 10 and csat/DR comparison (CB SCS
vs local MP seller) on page 11.
For CB MP seller launch, loop @Yiru Zhao <yiru.zhao@shopee.com> to
coordinate between BPM and Derek, will update next week.

@Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com> and @Cheng Derek <derek.cheng@shopee.com> ,
BPM collected cnls sellers' marketing feature needs (page 5 to 8), will
review and request to Bingyu team next week, see if good to you, and will
work with you on priority analysis

Thanks

content.question:
Thanks. + @Ning Li <ning.li@shopee.com>



Slide 10

   - How does the LLM + NLU co-exist?

- If we look at deflection rate and CSAT, how does the LLM compare with
   NLU at the moment?
- What is the plan moving forward for all 3 business lines?

Will we be
   using LLM or NLU or both and when?

Slide 11

   - I am not sure it is one or the other but a balance
   - If I have a question, I am perfectly ok if a bot can answer it. But if
   I find that you are avoiding me on purpose, naturally I will not be very
   happy about it. Off-hand, it does seem like our deflection rate is too high
   at the moment
   - Who is looking at this exactly?

On how we can improve our shopbot for
   SCS/Lovito/SIP?

- For eg. Do we know what are the most common queries that are coming
   into Shopbot right now for each business?

And do we have the relevant
   answers to feed to the bot?

Hi Ian


See answers below:


Slide 10

   -

   How does the LLM + NLU co-exist?
   -

      LLM is mostly used in pre-sales scenarios, while for post-sales
      enquires, it will more rely on NLU (directly get fixed answer from biz
      maintained intent knowledge base)
      -

   If we look at deflection rate and CSAT, how does the LLM compare with
   NLU at the moment?
   -

      Neither product team nor us did a fair AB testing before. If we want
      to make an objective comparison, it would be best to wait for
the A/B test
      for choice/lovito/SIP LLM rollout at the end of March.



   -

   What is the plan moving forward for all 3 business lines? Will we be
   using LLM or NLU or both and when?
   -

      SCS/Lovito/SIP shopbot will all use LLM+NLU combination
      -

      Rollout plan:
      -

         Now - March 19: A/B  test preparation, solution review &
         customization, UAT
         -

         Late March - Early April: A/B testing, LLM performance evaluation
         -

         Late April:  fully rollout for SCS/Lovito/SIP



Slide 11

   -

   I am not sure it is one or the other but a balance
   -

      It's supposed to be a balance
      -

   If I have a question, I am perfectly ok if a bot can answer it. But if I
   find that you are avoiding me on purpose, naturally I will not be very
   happy about it. Off-hand, it does seem like our deflection rate is too high
   at the moment
   -

      Yes, agree that choice BR/PH/VN deflection rate is a bit too high.
      Actually when we start to replace leliao(3rd party bot) with LLM shopbot
      for SCS on 2024.10, SCS CS Biz have done a manual sampling & tagging of
      Leliao and shopee bot deflected sessions  to verify the 'real deflection
      rate'( bot truly understand buyer intent and provide the right answer
      before buyer leave) of these two bots and it turns out the true
      deflection rat
